article_title: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
obligation_text: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way as to ensure that their operation is sufficiently transparent to enable deployers to interpret a system's output and use it appropriately.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI system transparency design, including interpretability mechanisms, output explanation methods, and design approaches that enable deployers to appropriately interpret and use AI system outputs.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI system transparency design, including interpretability mechanisms, output explanation methods, and design approaches that enable deployers to appropriately interpret and use AI system outputs.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of responsible AI principles and their operationalization, including fairness metrics, explainability methods, human oversight requirements, and AI impact assessment methodologies.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 13(1) (obligation) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-004 is the strongest match (STS: 0.687, cosine: 0.635). Secondary: AG-T-009 (AG, cosine: 0.612), RM-T-004 (RM, cosine: 0.611).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.9/10 (raw 0.687). Shared vocabulary: [ai, knowledge, methods]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [approaches, appropriately, deployers, design, enable, explanation] vs WIDAI terms [assessment, explainability, fairness, human, impact, methodologies] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.898 (P=0.893, R=0.903). Balanced P/R — the 26-word competency implication and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.9%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.635 < STS 0.687 (gap -0.052). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning between [approaches, appropriately, deployers] and [assessment, explainability, fairness] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 17.1%. Shared tokens: [ai, knowledge, methods]. EU AI Act-only: [approaches, appropriately, deployers, design, enable, explanation]. WIDAI-only: [assessment, explainability, fairness, human, impact, methodologies].
